title: Emphasis without exaggeration
subtitle: Especially, particularly, in fact.
overview: Module: Natural Language and Style. B2 Bridge moves from strong B1 output into abstract thinking, source reaction, nuanced opinions, natural style, and longer spoken independence.

sections.title: B2 bridge focus
rows: Good emphasis strengthens meaning without sounding dramatic.
好的强调能加强意思，而不显得夸张。
B2 bridge frame: choose precise words, use natural chunks, control tone, and reduce translation.
B2 Bridge 框架：选择准确词汇、使用自然语块、控制语气，并减少翻译。
Pronunciation is particularly important for learners who understand written English but struggle in conversation. In fact, rhythm may be the missing link between knowing and speaking.
发音对能理解书面英语但对话困难的学习者尤其重要。事实上，节奏可能是知道和说出来之间缺失的一环。

items.en: Good emphasis strengthens meaning without sounding dramatic.
items.zh: 好的强调能加强意思，而不显得夸张。

items.en: B2 bridge frame: choose precise words, use natural chunks, control tone, and reduce translation.
items.zh: B2 Bridge 框架：选择准确词汇、使用自然语块、控制语气，并减少翻译。

items.en: Pronunciation is particularly important for learners who understand written English but struggle in conversation. In fact, rhythm may be the missing link between knowing and speaking.
items.zh: 发音对能理解书面英语但对话困难的学习者尤其重要。事实上，节奏可能是知道和说出来之间缺失的一环。

items.en: I will not rush to sound advanced; I will make the idea clear first.
items.zh: 我不会急着显得高级；我会先把意思说清楚。

items.en: Then I will add nuance: a condition, a limit, a contrast, or a counterpoint.
items.zh: 然后我会加入细腻度：条件、限制、对比或反方观点。

items.en: If the sentence feels too heavy, I can paraphrase it in simpler spoken English.
items.zh: 如果句子感觉太沉重，我可以用更简单的口语英语改说。

items.en: A B2 answer should sound organized, flexible, and alive.
items.zh: B2 回答应该听起来有组织、灵活、有生命力。

items.en: I will record one answer and improve the structure, not only the grammar.
items.zh: 我会录一个回答，并改进结构，而不只是语法。

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: Pronunciation is particularly important for learners who understand written English but struggle in conversation. In fact, rhythm may be the missing link between knowing and speaking.
lines.zh: 发音对能理解书面英语但对话困难的学习者尤其重要。事实上，节奏可能是知道和说出来之间缺失的一环。

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: Can you add nuance or a limitation?
lines.zh: 你能加入细腻度或限制条件吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: Yes. I will add a condition, a contrast, or one fair counterpoint.
lines.zh: 可以。我会加入一个条件、一个对比或一个公平的反方观点。

items.en: Answer once in four sentences, then expand it to six or seven sentences.
items.zh: 先用四句话回答一次，然后扩展到六七句话。

items.en: Add one phrase that shows nuance: generally, however, that said, or it depends.
items.zh: 加入一个显示细腻度的短语：generally、however、that said 或 it depends。

items.en: Record the answer and check whether the structure is easy to follow.
items.zh: 录下回答，并检查结构是否容易跟上。

items.en: Reuse the best sentence tomorrow in a new topic.
items.zh: 明天在一个新话题中再次使用最好的句子。
